name: modal
tagline: Reference skill for deploying and scaling Python/ML workloads (GPUs, web endpoints, cron jobs) on Modal's serverless cloud.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped SDK reference for Modal's serverless cloud. Credential handling is explicit and minimal (only MODAL_TOKEN_ID/SECRET, with an explicit instruction not to read or forward other env vars), and it gives sound guidance on isolating untrusted code via Sandbox + egress allowlists and on avoiding shell-injection in subprocess calls. Only minor gap

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do NOT use this skill when...' guidance is given anywhere, so a user could misapply it to non-Modal or non-cloud deployment scenarios without a clear signal to stop.
Failure-path handling is thin: only a GPU fallback chain (lines 143-146) and generic timeout config (line 294) are covered; no guidance on handling deploy failures, auth errors, or quota/OOM issues.
